title: carex remota subsp. remota
intro: Carex remota subsp. remota, also known as the wood sedge, is a species of flowering plant in the sedge family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and infections. The health benefits of wood sedge include its ability to reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various skin conditions and injuries. In herbal preparations, wood sedge is often used in infusions, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from skin issues.
